root_text.an4.185:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.an4.185:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā abhiññātā abhiññātā paribbājakā sippinikātīre paribbājakārāme paṭivasanti, seyyathidaṁ annabhāro varadharo sakuludāyī ca paribbājako aññe ca abhiññātā abhiññātā paribbājakā.
root_text.an4.185:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena sippinikātīre paribbājakārāmo tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.an4.185:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarā kathā udapādi:
root_text.an4.185:2.3: Atha kho bhagavā yena te paribbājakā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho bhagavā te paribbājake etadavoca:
root_text.an4.185:3.1: “Kāya nuttha, paribbājakā, etarahi kathāya sannisinnā, kā ca pana vo antarākathā vippakatā”ti?
root_text.an4.185:3.2: “Idha, bho gotama, amhākaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.an4.185:4.1: “Cattārimāni, paribbājakā, brāhmaṇasaccāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditāni.
root_text.an4.185:4.5: Iti vadaṁ brāhmaṇo saccaṁ āha, no musā.
root_text.an4.185:4.6: So tena na samaṇoti maññati, na brāhmaṇoti maññati, na seyyohamasmīti maññati, na sadisohamasmīti maññati, na hīnohamasmīti maññati.
root_text.an4.185:4.7: Api ca yadeva tattha saccaṁ tadabhiññāya pāṇānaṁyeva anuddayāya anukampāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.185:5.3: Iti vadaṁ brāhmaṇo saccamāha, no musā.
root_text.an4.185:5.4: So tena na samaṇoti maññati, na brāhmaṇoti maññati, na seyyohamasmīti maññati, na sadisohamasmīti maññati, na hīnohamasmīti maññati.
root_text.an4.185:5.5: Api ca yadeva tattha saccaṁ tadabhiññāya kāmānaṁyeva nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.185:6.2: ‘sabbe bhavā aniccā …pe… tadabhiññāya bhavānaṁyeva nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.185:7.2: ‘nāhaṁ kvacani kassaci kiñcanatasmiṁ na ca mama kvacani katthaci kiñcanatatthī’ti.
root_text.an4.185:7.3: Iti vadaṁ brāhmaṇo saccaṁ āha, no musā.
root_text.an4.185:7.4: So tena na samaṇoti maññati, na brāhmaṇoti maññati, na seyyohamasmīti maññati, na sadisohamasmīti maññati, na hīnohamasmīti maññati.
root_text.an4.185:7.5: Api ca yadeva tattha saccaṁ tadabhiññāya ākiñcaññaṁyeva paṭipadaṁ paṭipanno hoti.
root_text.an4.185:7.6: Imāni kho, paribbājakā, cattāri brāhmaṇasaccāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditānī”ti.

translation_text.an4.185:1.1: Once the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.an4.185:1.2: Now at that time several very well-known wanderers were residing in the monastery of the wanderers on the bank of the Serpentine river. They included Annabhāra, Varadhara, Sakuludāyī, and other very well-known wanderers.
translation_text.an4.185:1.3: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to the wanderer’s monastery on the bank of the Serpentine river.
translation_text.an4.185:2.1: Now at that time this discussion came up while those wanderers of other religions were sitting together,
translation_text.an4.185:2.2: “The truths of the brahmins are like this; the truths of the brahmins are like that.”
translation_text.an4.185:2.3: Then the Buddha went up to those wanderers, sat down on the seat spread out, and said to them,
translation_text.an4.185:3.1: “Wanderers, what were you sitting talking about just now? What conversation was left unfinished?”
translation_text.an4.185:3.2: “Well, worthy Gotama, this discussion came up among us while we were sitting together:
translation_text.an4.185:3.3: ‘The truths of the brahmins are like this; the truths of the brahmins are like that.’”
translation_text.an4.185:4.1: “Wanderers, I declare these four truths of the brahmins, having realized them with my own insight.
translation_text.an4.185:4.5: In speaking like this, a brahmin speaks the truth, not lies.
translation_text.an4.185:4.6: But they don’t think of themselves as an ‘ascetic’ or ‘brahmin’ because of that. Nor do they think ‘I’m better’ or ‘I’m equal’ or ‘I’m worse’.
translation_text.an4.185:4.7: Rather, they simply practice out of kindness and sympathy for living creatures, having had insight into the truth of that.
translation_text.an4.185:5.2: ‘All sensual pleasures are impermanent, suffering, and perishable.’
translation_text.an4.185:5.3: Saying this, a brahmin speaks the truth, not lies.
translation_text.an4.185:5.4: But they don’t think of themselves as an ‘ascetic’ or ‘brahmin’ because of that. Nor do they think ‘I’m better’ or ‘I’m equal’ or ‘I’m worse’.
translation_text.an4.185:5.5: Rather, they simply practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding sensual pleasures, having had insight into the truth of that.
translation_text.an4.185:6.2: ‘All states of existence are impermanent, suffering, and perishable.’ … They simply practice for disillusionment, dispassion, and cessation regarding future lives, having had insight into the truth of that.
translation_text.an4.185:7.2: ‘I don’t belong to anyone anywhere. And nothing belongs to me anywhere.’
translation_text.an4.185:7.3: In speaking like this, a brahmin speaks the truth, not lies.
translation_text.an4.185:7.4: But they don’t think of themselves as an ‘ascetic’ or ‘brahmin’ because of that. Nor do they think ‘I’m better’ or ‘I’m equal’ or ‘I’m worse’.
translation_text.an4.185:7.5: Rather, they simply practice the path of nothingness, having had insight into the truth of that.
translation_text.an4.185:7.6: These are the four truths of the brahmins that I declare, having realized them with my own insight.”

variant_text.an4.185:4.7: pāṇānaṁyeva anuddayāya → pāṇānaṁyeva anudayāya (sya-all); tadabhiññāya anudayāya (mr)
